company: Resonance Health Analysis Services Pty Ltd
sensitivity: 96% (at 1.8 mg Fe/g), 96% (at 3.0 mg Fe/g), 94% (at 3.2 mg Fe/g), 91% (at 5.0 mg Fe/g), 92% (at 7.0 mg Fe/g), 89% (at 15.0 mg Fe/g)
specificity: 80% (at 1.8 mg Fe/g), 95% (at 3.0 mg Fe/g), 95% (at 3.2 mg Fe/g), 97% (at 5.0 mg Fe/g), 97% (at 7.0 mg Fe/g), 98% (at 15.0 mg Fe/g)
intended_use: For the measurement of R2 and iron concentration in the liver from MRI scans.
intended_use_full: For the measurement of R2 and iron concentration in the liver from MRI scans.
target_condition: Systemic iron overload, including transfusion-dependent thalassemia, sickle cell disease, non-transfusion-dependent thalassemia, and hereditary hemochromatosis
ground_truth: FerriScan R2-MRI Analysis System (predicate device); literature also references biopsy standard error of 19% to >40% for comparison
other_metrics: Repeatability study (60 subjects with repeat scans): 95% limits of repeatability ratios of 1.26 (95% CI 1.24-1.28) and 0.79 (95% CI 0.78-0.81) above 3 mg Fe/g dry tissue, corresponding to approximately 9% standard error on single measurement. Bias between FerriSmart and FerriScan is negligible below 3 mg Fe/g dry tissue and clinically acceptable above.
